research_question: Can a key-disjoint atlas learned from exact propagation-amplification signatures of known low20 keys rank the hidden low20 key of the frozen standard-output R20 target?

breadcrumb.interpretation_fixed_before_A214_measure: hard first-order closure does not reach another key variable, while propagation volume and topology vary strongly enough to define a prospective soft amplification surface

known_key_atlas.fixed_public_material: reuse the target high12 bits of key word0, key words1-through7, counter schedule, nonce, block count, and standard R20 implementation
known_key_atlas.vary_only: low20 of key word0 and the resulting eight target blocks
known_key_atlas.target_collision_policy: if the independently revealed target low20 equals any atlas key, retain TARGET_KEY_COLLISION_BOUNDARY and make no unseen-key transfer claim

probe_cover_per_challenge.triples: not used in model fitting; after the target candidate order is frozen, exact triple probes may be applied only to a fixed top-k shortlist in a separately labeled extension

feature_families.input: packbits(newly_assigned_variable_mask) with an exact fixed header
feature_families.features: compressed bytes and delta from the base-mask code length for each codec
correlation-aware rank aggregation of F1, F2, F3, F4, and their combined model; no product of p-values

learning_protocol.unit_of_independence: complete known key, never an individual probe row
row_tasks: unary assumption matches the known key bit
pair assumption matches both known key bits
learning_protocol.model: standardized ridge logistic regression fitted by deterministic L-BFGS
learning_protocol.selection_objective: lexicographic median true-key rank, hit_at_1024, mean reciprocal rank across validation keys
learning_protocol.candidate_score: sum of the fitted unary logit for each of 20 selected bit values plus the fitted pair logit for each of 190 selected bit-pair values
learning_protocol.consensus: mean within-target percentile rank across the five frozen model orders
learning_protocol.label_null: 64 deterministic nonzero circular rotations of complete training-key labels; never shuffle probe rows within a key

target_reveal_barrier.before_reveal_write: model coefficients, scalers, validation metrics, five complete target candidate-order SHA256 values, consensus candidate-order SHA256, and top-1024 lists
target_reveal_barrier.reveal_method_after_freeze: independent exhaustive standard ChaCha20 R20 comparison over 2^20 low20 values

predeclared_readout.strong_transfer: unseen target key ranks at most 1024 in the frozen consensus and validation keys beat the complete-key label null
predeclared_readout.direct_rank1_transfer: unseen target key ranks first in the frozen consensus and passes all 4096 output bits while the flipped control rejects
predeclared_readout.useful_domain_reduction: unseen target key lies in any predeclared top-k below the full domain
predeclared_readout.representation_boundary: validation or target ranking is indistinguishable from the complete-key label null; retain which feature families and assumption arities fail
